key_insight: A fully discrete least-squares splitting framework can be proven convergent for the 2D Monge-Ampère equation when combined with finite element Hessian reconstruction and a local determinant projection.
engineering_relevance: This gives a rigorous path to stable, high-order finite element solvers for nonlinear PDEs used in geometry, optimal transport, and related computational mechanics problems.
method: Reconstruct the Hessian with finite elements.
Project locally onto the Monge-Ampère determinant constraint.
Use discrete Miranda-Talenti estimates to prove local convergence and optimal H^2-type convergence.
evidence_signal: The abstract reports optimal-order convergence of the limit solution and numerical experiments that confirm the predicted convergence rates.
watch_item: Convergence is local and relies on a discrete Miranda-Talenti estimate; some lower-order C0-IP/DG cases require sufficiently small |u|_{H^3(Ω)}.
paper_title: Fully discrete least-squares splitting scheme for the Monge-Ampère equation: finite element analysis and convergence
